sourceMovie.title: Crank
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Crank (2006) as a action theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The action and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Transporter 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Crank clicked for you, Transporter 2 (2005) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: 6 Underground
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Crank clicked for you, 6 Underground (2019) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and thriller, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

If Crank clicked for you, 11:14 (2004) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller and crime, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Crank?
faq.answer: Try Transporter 2, Desperado, Run All Night—each shares DNA with Crank while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Crank?
faq.answer: Crank (2006) is best described as Action, Thriller, Crime. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Crank part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Crank Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
